content.body:
> <@akirose:matrix.org> I'm at a conference this week so I couldn't have joined yesterday, but I could drop in on the 29 April meeting. Lmk when you have a new agenda posted for me to add myself, or feel free to add a line like "understanding and responding to cybersecurity regulation"

Does that mean overlap between security regulation and standards work?
content.formatted_body:
In reply to @akirose:matrix.org
I'm at a conference this week so I couldn't have joined yesterday, but I could drop in on the 29 April meeting. Lmk when you have a new agenda posted for me to add myself, or feel free to add a line like "understanding and responding to cybersecurity regulation"
Does that mean overlap between security regulation and standards work?

content.body: but standards work perhaps more generically than what ECMA-262 publishes. Cybersecurity regulation that will impact functionally all programmers everywhere, but which I haven't quite pinpointed if it does indeed matter to TC39.
(nb I am working on these standards as well outside of my work for Ecma, though the current curiosity is on behalf of Ecma)

Found the agenda doc. I think that's the one. It was a bit outdated https://docs.google.com/document/d/1FZ95-NZIQE9fw3A8Sgcz2BKep6MlC_Kng0dlf1ehabQ
LMK if you have access. I've added you to the agenda for tomorrow.
